sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।एवं वृष्ण्यन्धककुले श्रुत्वा मौसलमाहवम् ।पाण्डवाः किमकुर्वन्त तथा कृष्णे दिवं गते ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,evaṁ vṛṣṇyandhakakule śrutvā mausalamāhavam ,pāṇḍavāḥ kimakurvanta tathā kṛṣṇe divaṁ gate.
english: Janamejaya asked: "After hearing about the mace-battle that befell the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans in this manner, what did the Pāṇḍavas do, especially after Kṛṣṇa had ascended to the heavens?"
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 1. Janamejaya asked: "After hearing about the mace-battle that befell the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans in this manner, what did the Pāṇḍavas do, especially after Kṛṣṇa had ascended to the heavens?"

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।श्रुत्वैव कौरवो राजा वृष्णीनां कदनं महत् ।प्रस्थाने मतिमाधाय वाक्यमर्जुनमब्रवीत् ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śrutvaiva kauravo rājā vṛṣṇīnāṁ kadanaṁ mahat ,prasthāne matimādhāya vākyamarjunamabravīt.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Indeed, as soon as the Kuru king (Yudhiṣṭhira) heard of the great calamity that befell the Vṛṣṇis, he set his mind on a great departure (mahāprasthāna) and spoke to Arjuna."
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 2. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Indeed, as soon as the Kuru king (Yudhiṣṭhira) heard of the great calamity that befell the Vṛṣṇis, he set his mind on a great departure (mahāprasthāna) and spoke to Arjuna."

sanskrit: कालः पचति भूतानि सर्वाण्येव महामते ।कर्मन्यासमहं मन्ये त्वमपि द्रष्टुमर्हसि ॥३॥
transliteration: kālaḥ pacati bhūtāni sarvāṇyeva mahāmate ,karmanyāsamahaṁ manye tvamapi draṣṭumarhasi.
english: O great-minded one, time (kāla) certainly consumes all beings. I consider the renunciation of actions (karma) [to be proper], and you too should comprehend this.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 3. O great-minded one, time (kāla) certainly consumes all beings. I consider the renunciation of actions (karma) [to be proper], and you too should comprehend this.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तु कौन्तेयः कालः काल इति ब्रुवन् ।अन्वपद्यत तद्वाक्यं भ्रातुर्ज्येष्ठस्य वीर्यवान् ॥४॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tu kaunteyaḥ kālaḥ kāla iti bruvan ,anvapadyata tadvākyaṁ bhrāturjyeṣṭhasya vīryavān.
english: Having been addressed thus, that valorous son of Kunti (Arjuna), exclaiming 'Time (kāla)! Time (kāla)!', accepted the statement of his elder brother.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 4. Having been addressed thus, that valorous son of Kunti (Arjuna), exclaiming 'Time (kāla)! Time (kāla)!', accepted the statement of his elder brother.

sanskrit: अर्जुनस्य मतं ज्ञात्वा भीमसेनो यमौ तथा ।अन्वपद्यन्त तद्वाक्यं यदुक्तं सव्यसाचिना ॥५॥
transliteration: arjunasya mataṁ jñātvā bhīmaseno yamau tathā ,anvapadyanta tadvākyaṁ yaduktaṁ savyasācinā.
english: Having understood Arjuna's opinion, Bhīmasena and the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) likewise accepted that statement which was uttered by Savyasācin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 5. Having understood Arjuna's opinion, Bhīmasena and the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) likewise accepted that statement which was uttered by Savyasācin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: ततो युयुत्सुमानाय्य प्रव्रजन्धर्मकाम्यया ।राज्यं परिददौ सर्वं वैश्यापुत्रे युधिष्ठिरः ॥६॥
transliteration: tato yuyutsumānāyya pravrajandharmakāmyayā ,rājyaṁ paridadau sarvaṁ vaiśyāputre yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Then, out of a desire for righteousness (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira, having summoned Yuyutsu, handed over the entire kingdom to the son of the Vaiśya woman (Yuyutsu), as he himself embarked on his ascetic journey.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 6. Then, out of a desire for righteousness (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira, having summoned Yuyutsu, handed over the entire kingdom to the son of the Vaiśya woman (Yuyutsu), as he himself embarked on his ascetic journey.

sanskrit: अभिषिच्य स्वराज्ये तु तं राजानं परिक्षितम् ।दुःखार्तश्चाब्रवीद्राजा सुभद्रां पाण्डवाग्रजः ॥७॥
transliteration: abhiṣicya svarājye tu taṁ rājānaṁ parikṣitam ,duḥkhārtaścābravīdrājā subhadrāṁ pāṇḍavāgrajaḥ.
english: Having consecrated King Parikṣit in his own kingdom, the eldest of the Pāṇḍavas, Yudhiṣṭhira, himself distressed by sorrow, then spoke to Subhadrā.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 7. Having consecrated King Parikṣit in his own kingdom, the eldest of the Pāṇḍavas, Yudhiṣṭhira, himself distressed by sorrow, then spoke to Subhadrā.

sanskrit: एष पुत्रस्य ते पुत्रः कुरुराजो भविष्यति ।यदूनां परिशेषश्च वज्रो राजा कृतश्च ह ॥८॥
transliteration: eṣa putrasya te putraḥ kururājo bhaviṣyati ,yadūnāṁ pariśeṣaśca vajro rājā kṛtaśca ha.
english: This grandson of yours, the son of your son, will be the king of the Kurus. And Vajra has been appointed king, being the sole survivor of the Yadus.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 8. This grandson of yours, the son of your son, will be the king of the Kurus. And Vajra has been appointed king, being the sole survivor of the Yadus.

sanskrit: परिक्षिद्धास्तिनपुरे शक्रप्रस्थे तु यादवः ।वज्रो राजा त्वया रक्ष्यो मा चाधर्मे मनः कृथाः ॥९॥
transliteration: parikṣiddhāstinapure śakraprasthe tu yādavaḥ ,vajro rājā tvayā rakṣyo mā cādharme manaḥ kṛthāḥ.
english: Parikṣit will rule in Hastinapura. As for the Yādava king Vajra, he should be protected by you in Śakraprastha. And do not ever set your mind on unrighteousness (adharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 9. Parikṣit will rule in Hastinapura. As for the Yādava king Vajra, he should be protected by you in Śakraprastha. And do not ever set your mind on unrighteousness (adharma).

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा धर्मराजः स वासुदेवस्य धीमतः ।मातुलस्य च वृद्धस्य रामादीनां तथैव च ॥१०॥
transliteration: ityuktvā dharmarājaḥ sa vāsudevasya dhīmataḥ ,mātulasya ca vṛddhasya rāmādīnāṁ tathaiva ca.
english: Thus having spoken, that king of natural law (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira, also gave thought to the wise Vāsudeva, and the aged maternal uncle, and Balarāma and others as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 10. Thus having spoken, that king of natural law (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira, also gave thought to the wise Vāsudeva, and the aged maternal uncle, and Balarāma and others as well.

sanskrit: भ्रातृभिः सह धर्मात्मा कृत्वोदकमतन्द्रितः ।श्राद्धान्युद्दिश्य सर्वेषां चकार विधिवत्तदा ॥११॥
transliteration: bhrātṛbhiḥ saha dharmātmā kṛtvodakamatandritaḥ ,śrāddhānyuddiśya sarveṣāṁ cakāra vidhivattadā.
english: Then, the righteous (dharma-abiding) Yudhiṣṭhira, diligently along with his brothers, performed the water rites and offered the śrāddha ceremonies for all (ancestors) according to the prescribed rules.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 11. Then, the righteous (dharma-abiding) Yudhiṣṭhira, diligently along with his brothers, performed the water rites and offered the śrāddha ceremonies for all (ancestors) according to the prescribed rules.

sanskrit: ददौ रत्नानि वासांसि ग्रामानश्वान्रथानपि ।स्त्रियश्च द्विजमुख्येभ्यो गवां शतसहस्रशः ॥१२॥
transliteration: dadau ratnāni vāsāṁsi grāmānaśvānrathānapi ,striyaśca dvijamukhyebhyo gavāṁ śatasahasraśaḥ.
english: He gave jewels, garments, villages, horses, and chariots, as well as female attendants and hundreds of thousands of cows, to the chief among the twice-born (dvija) ones.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 12. He gave jewels, garments, villages, horses, and chariots, as well as female attendants and hundreds of thousands of cows, to the chief among the twice-born (dvija) ones.

sanskrit: कृपमभ्यर्च्य च गुरुमर्थमानपुरस्कृतम् ।शिष्यं परिक्षितं तस्मै ददौ भरतसत्तमः ॥१३॥
transliteration: kṛpamabhyarcya ca gurumarthamānapuraskṛtam ,śiṣyaṁ parikṣitaṁ tasmai dadau bharatasattamaḥ.
english: And the best of the Bhāratas, having worshipped his preceptor Kṛpa, who was honored with wealth and respect, entrusted his disciple Parikṣit to him (Kṛpa).
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 13. And the best of the Bhāratas, having worshipped his preceptor Kṛpa, who was honored with wealth and respect, entrusted his disciple Parikṣit to him (Kṛpa).

sanskrit: ततस्तु प्रकृतीः सर्वाः समानाय्य युधिष्ठिरः ।सर्वमाचष्ट राजर्षिश्चिकीर्षितमथात्मनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tatastu prakṛtīḥ sarvāḥ samānāyya yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,sarvamācaṣṭa rājarṣiścikīrṣitamathātmanaḥ.
english: Then, having assembled all the ministers and subjects, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the royal sage, declared his own intentions.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 14. Then, having assembled all the ministers and subjects, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the royal sage, declared his own intentions.

sanskrit: ते श्रुत्वैव वचस्तस्य पौरजानपदा जनाः ।भृशमुद्विग्नमनसो नाभ्यनन्दन्त तद्वचः ॥१५॥
transliteration: te śrutvaiva vacastasya paurajānapadā janāḥ ,bhṛśamudvignamanaso nābhyanandanta tadvacaḥ.
english: Upon hearing his words, the people of the city and the countryside, deeply distressed in their minds, did not approve of those words.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 15. Upon hearing his words, the people of the city and the countryside, deeply distressed in their minds, did not approve of those words.

sanskrit: नैवं कर्तव्यमिति ते तदोचुस्ते नराधिपम् ।न च राजा तथाकार्षीत्कालपर्यायधर्मवित् ॥१६॥
transliteration: naivaṁ kartavyamiti te tadocuste narādhipam ,na ca rājā tathākārṣītkālaparyāyadharmavit.
english: "This should not be done," they said to the king then. However, the king, understanding the natural law (dharma) of the changing times, did not act in that manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 16. "This should not be done," they said to the king then. However, the king, understanding the natural law (dharma) of the changing times, did not act in that manner.

sanskrit: ततोऽनुमान्य धर्मात्मा पौरजानपदं जनम् ।गमनाय मतिं चक्रे भ्रातरश्चास्य ते तदा ॥१७॥
transliteration: tato'numānya dharmātmā paurajānapadaṁ janam ,gamanāya matiṁ cakre bhrātaraścāsya te tadā.
english: Then, the righteous-souled (dharma) one, having appeased the people of the city and the countryside, made up his mind for departure. And at that time, his brothers also did the same.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 17. Then, the righteous-souled (dharma) one, having appeased the people of the city and the countryside, made up his mind for departure. And at that time, his brothers also did the same.

sanskrit: ततः स राजा कौरव्यो धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।उत्सृज्याभरणान्यङ्गाज्जगृहे वल्कलान्युत ॥१८॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa rājā kauravyo dharmaputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,utsṛjyābharaṇānyaṅgājjagṛhe valkalānyuta.
english: Then, that King Yudhiṣṭhira, the descendant of Kuru and son of (dharma), casting off the ornaments from his body, indeed took up bark garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 18. Then, that King Yudhiṣṭhira, the descendant of Kuru and son of (dharma), casting off the ornaments from his body, indeed took up bark garments.

sanskrit: भीमार्जुनौ यमौ चैव द्रौपदी च यशस्विनी ।तथैव सर्वे जगृहुर्वल्कलानि जनाधिप ॥१९॥
transliteration: bhīmārjunau yamau caiva draupadī ca yaśasvinī ,tathaiva sarve jagṛhurvalkalāni janādhipa.
english: O King of men, Bhima and Arjuna, the two Yamas, and the glorious Draupadi, as well as all the others, similarly took bark garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 19. O King of men, Bhima and Arjuna, the two Yamas, and the glorious Draupadi, as well as all the others, similarly took bark garments.

sanskrit: विधिवत्कारयित्वेष्टिं नैष्ठिकीं भरतर्षभ ।समुत्सृज्याप्सु सर्वेऽग्नीन्प्रतस्थुर्नरपुंगवाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: vidhivatkārayitveṣṭiṁ naiṣṭhikīṁ bharatarṣabha ,samutsṛjyāpsu sarve'gnīnpratasthurnarapuṁgavāḥ.
english: O best of Bharatas, after having the final (Vedic ritual) (iṣṭi) performed according to the rules, and having dissolved all their sacred fires into the waters, those excellent men departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 20. O best of Bharatas, after having the final (Vedic ritual) (iṣṭi) performed according to the rules, and having dissolved all their sacred fires into the waters, those excellent men departed.

sanskrit: ततः प्ररुरुदुः सर्वाः स्त्रियो दृष्ट्वा नरर्षभान् ।प्रस्थितान्द्रौपदीषष्ठान्पुरा द्यूतजितान्यथा ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ praruruduḥ sarvāḥ striyo dṛṣṭvā nararṣabhān ,prasthitāndraupadīṣaṣṭhānpurā dyūtajitānyathā.
english: Then, all the women burst into tears upon seeing those excellent men, with Draupadi as the sixth, departing, just as they had been defeated in the dice game before.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 21. Then, all the women burst into tears upon seeing those excellent men, with Draupadi as the sixth, departing, just as they had been defeated in the dice game before.

sanskrit: हर्षोऽभवच्च सर्वेषां भ्रातॄणां गमनं प्रति ।युधिष्ठिरमतं ज्ञात्वा वृष्णिक्षयमवेक्ष्य च ॥२२॥
transliteration: harṣo'bhavacca sarveṣāṁ bhrātṝṇāṁ gamanaṁ prati ,yudhiṣṭhiramataṁ jñātvā vṛṣṇikṣayamavekṣya ca.
english: And a feeling of joy arose in all the brothers concerning their departure, as they had understood Yudhishthira's resolve and had witnessed the destruction of the Vrishnis.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 22. And a feeling of joy arose in all the brothers concerning their departure, as they had understood Yudhishthira's resolve and had witnessed the destruction of the Vrishnis.

sanskrit: भ्रातरः पञ्च कृष्णा च षष्ठी श्वा चैव सप्तमः ।आत्मना सप्तमो राजा निर्ययौ गजसाह्वयात् ।पौरैरनुगतो दूरं सर्वैरन्तःपुरैस्तथा ॥२३॥
transliteration: bhrātaraḥ pañca kṛṣṇā ca ṣaṣṭhī śvā caiva saptamaḥ ,ātmanā saptamo rājā niryayau gajasāhvayāt ,paurairanugato dūraṁ sarvairantaḥpuraistathā.
english: The five brothers, along with Krishna (Draupadi) as the sixth and a dog as the seventh – with the king himself (ātmanā) being the seventh among them – departed from Hastinapura. He was followed a long way by all the citizens and all the women of the inner palace.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 23. The five brothers, along with Krishna (Draupadi) as the sixth and a dog as the seventh – with the king himself (ātmanā) being the seventh among them – departed from Hastinapura. He was followed a long way by all the citizens and all the women of the inner palace.

sanskrit: न चैनमशकत्कश्चिन्निवर्तस्वेति भाषितुम् ।न्यवर्तन्त ततः सर्वे नरा नगरवासिनः ॥२४॥
transliteration: na cainamaśakatkaścinnivartasveti bhāṣitum ,nyavartanta tataḥ sarve narā nagaravāsinaḥ.
english: And no one was able to tell him, 'Turn back!' Then, all the city-dwelling men returned from there.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 24. And no one was able to tell him, 'Turn back!' Then, all the city-dwelling men returned from there.

sanskrit: कृपप्रभृतयश्चैव युयुत्सुं पर्यवारयन् ।विवेश गङ्गां कौरव्य उलूपी भुजगात्मजा ॥२५॥
transliteration: kṛpaprabhṛtayaścaiva yuyutsuṁ paryavārayan ,viveśa gaṅgāṁ kauravya ulūpī bhujagātmajā.
english: Kripa and others surrounded Yuyutsu, the Kaurava. Ulupi, daughter of the serpent (bhujagātmajā), entered the Ganga.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 25. Kripa and others surrounded Yuyutsu, the Kaurava. Ulupi, daughter of the serpent (bhujagātmajā), entered the Ganga.

sanskrit: चित्राङ्गदा ययौ चापि मणिपूरपुरं प्रति ।शिष्टाः परिक्षितं त्वन्या मातरः पर्यवारयन् ॥२६॥
transliteration: citrāṅgadā yayau cāpi maṇipūrapuraṁ prati ,śiṣṭāḥ parikṣitaṁ tvanyā mātaraḥ paryavārayan.
english: And Chitrangada also went to the city of Manipura. But the other remaining mothers surrounded Parikshit.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 26. And Chitrangada also went to the city of Manipura. But the other remaining mothers surrounded Parikshit.

sanskrit: पाण्डवाश्च महात्मानो द्रौपदी च यशस्विनी ।कृतोपवासाः कौरव्य प्रययुः प्राङ्मुखास्ततः ॥२७॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāśca mahātmāno draupadī ca yaśasvinī ,kṛtopavāsāḥ kauravya prayayuḥ prāṅmukhāstataḥ.
english: O descendant of Kuru, then the great-souled Pāṇḍavas and the glorious Draupadī, having undertaken a fast, departed facing east.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 27. O descendant of Kuru, then the great-souled Pāṇḍavas and the glorious Draupadī, having undertaken a fast, departed facing east.

sanskrit: योगयुक्ता महात्मानस्त्यागधर्ममुपेयुषः ।अभिजग्मुर्बहून्देशान्सरितः पर्वतांस्तथा ॥२८॥
transliteration: yogayuktā mahātmānastyāgadharmamupeyuṣaḥ ,abhijagmurbahūndeśānsaritaḥ parvatāṁstathā.
english: The great-souled ones, absorbed in (yoga) and having embraced the natural law (dharma) of renunciation, journeyed through many countries, rivers, and mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 28. The great-souled ones, absorbed in (yoga) and having embraced the natural law (dharma) of renunciation, journeyed through many countries, rivers, and mountains.

english: Yudhiṣṭhira walked at the front, followed by Bhīma; Arjuna came immediately after him, and then the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) in due sequence.

sanskrit: पृष्ठतस्तु वरारोहा श्यामा पद्मदलेक्षणा ।द्रौपदी योषितां श्रेष्ठा ययौ भरतसत्तम ॥३०॥
transliteration: pṛṣṭhatastu varārohā śyāmā padmadalekṣaṇā ,draupadī yoṣitāṁ śreṣṭhā yayau bharatasattama.
english: But behind them, O best of Bharatas, Draupadī, a youthful woman with beautiful hips and lotus-petal eyes, the foremost among women, walked.

sanskrit: श्वा चैवानुययावेकः पाण्डवान्प्रस्थितान्वने ।क्रमेण ते ययुर्वीरा लौहित्यं सलिलार्णवम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: śvā caivānuyayāvekaḥ pāṇḍavānprasthitānvane ,krameṇa te yayurvīrā lauhityaṁ salilārṇavam.
english: A single dog indeed followed the Pandavas as they set out into the forest. In due course, those heroes reached the Lohitya, which was like an ocean of water.

sanskrit: गाण्डीवं च धनुर्दिव्यं न मुमोच धनंजयः ।रत्नलोभान्महाराज तौ चाक्षय्यौ महेषुधी ॥३२॥
transliteration: gāṇḍīvaṁ ca dhanurdivyaṁ na mumoca dhanaṁjayaḥ ,ratnalobhānmahārāja tau cākṣayyau maheṣudhī.
english: O great king, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) did not abandon the divine Gāṇḍīva bow, nor those two inexhaustible great quivers, out of attachment to their preciousness.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 32. O great king, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) did not abandon the divine Gāṇḍīva bow, nor those two inexhaustible great quivers, out of attachment to their preciousness.

sanskrit: अग्निं ते ददृशुस्तत्र स्थितं शैलमिवाग्रतः ।मार्गमावृत्य तिष्ठन्तं साक्षात्पुरुषविग्रहम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: agniṁ te dadṛśustatra sthitaṁ śailamivāgrataḥ ,mārgamāvṛtya tiṣṭhantaṁ sākṣātpuruṣavigraham.
english: There, they beheld Agni (the fire god) standing before them like a mountain, blocking the path, manifest directly in the form of a person (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 33. There, they beheld Agni (the fire god) standing before them like a mountain, blocking the path, manifest directly in the form of a person (puruṣa).

sanskrit: ततो देवः स सप्तार्चिः पाण्डवानिदमब्रवीत् ।भो भो पाण्डुसुता वीराः पावकं मां विबोधत ॥३४॥
transliteration: tato devaḥ sa saptārciḥ pāṇḍavānidamabravīt ,bho bho pāṇḍusutā vīrāḥ pāvakaṁ māṁ vibodhata.
english: Then that god, the seven-rayed Agni, said this to the Pandavas: "O heroic sons of Pāṇḍu, know me as Fire!"
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 34. Then that god, the seven-rayed Agni, said this to the Pandavas: "O heroic sons of Pāṇḍu, know me as Fire!"

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर महाबाहो भीमसेन परंतप ।अर्जुनाश्विसुतौ वीरौ निबोधत वचो मम ॥३५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira mahābāho bhīmasena paraṁtapa ,arjunāśvisutau vīrau nibodhata vaco mama.
english: O Yudhishthira, mighty-armed one! O Bhimasena, tormentor of enemies! O Arjuna and the valiant sons of the Ashvins, listen to my words.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 35. O Yudhishthira, mighty-armed one! O Bhimasena, tormentor of enemies! O Arjuna and the valiant sons of the Ashvins, listen to my words.

sanskrit: अहमग्निः कुरुश्रेष्ठा मया दग्धं च खाण्डवम् ।अर्जुनस्य प्रभावेण तथा नारायणस्य च ॥३६॥
transliteration: ahamagniḥ kuruśreṣṭhā mayā dagdhaṁ ca khāṇḍavam ,arjunasya prabhāveṇa tathā nārāyaṇasya ca.
english: I am Agni, O best among the Kurus! The Khandava forest was burnt by me, through the power of Arjuna and also of Narayana.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 36. I am Agni, O best among the Kurus! The Khandava forest was burnt by me, through the power of Arjuna and also of Narayana.

sanskrit: अयं वः फल्गुनो भ्राता गाण्डीवं परमायुधम् ।परित्यज्य वनं यातु नानेनार्थोऽस्ति कश्चन ॥३७॥
transliteration: ayaṁ vaḥ phalguno bhrātā gāṇḍīvaṁ paramāyudham ,parityajya vanaṁ yātu nānenārtho'sti kaścana.
english: Let this Arjuna, your brother, go to the forest, abandoning his supreme weapon, the Gaṇḍīva bow. There is no longer any purpose for him with this (weapon).
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 37. Let this Arjuna, your brother, go to the forest, abandoning his supreme weapon, the Gaṇḍīva bow. There is no longer any purpose for him with this (weapon).

sanskrit: चक्ररत्नं तु यत्कृष्णे स्थितमासीन्महात्मनि ।गतं तच्च पुनर्हस्ते कालेनैष्यति तस्य ह ॥३८॥
transliteration: cakraratnaṁ tu yatkṛṣṇe sthitamāsīnmahātmani ,gataṁ tacca punarhaste kālenaiṣyati tasya ha.
english: However, the jewel-like discus (cakraratna) that was present with the great-souled Krishna -  that too is now gone. Indeed, it will return to his hand in due time.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 38. However, the jewel-like discus (cakraratna) that was present with the great-souled Krishna -  that too is now gone. Indeed, it will return to his hand in due time.

sanskrit: वरुणादाहृतं पूर्वं मयैतत्पार्थकारणात् ।गाण्डीवं कार्मुकश्रेष्ठं वरुणायैव दीयताम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: varuṇādāhṛtaṁ pūrvaṁ mayaitatpārthakāraṇāt ,gāṇḍīvaṁ kārmukaśreṣṭhaṁ varuṇāyaiva dīyatām.
english: This Gāṇḍīva, the best of bows, was previously taken by me from Varuṇa for Arjuna's sake. Let it now be given back to Varuṇa himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 39. This Gāṇḍīva, the best of bows, was previously taken by me from Varuṇa for Arjuna's sake. Let it now be given back to Varuṇa himself.

sanskrit: ततस्ते भ्रातरः सर्वे धनंजयमचोदयन् ।स जले प्राक्षिपत्तत्तु तथाक्षय्यौ महेषुधी ॥४०॥
transliteration: tataste bhrātaraḥ sarve dhanaṁjayamacodayan ,sa jale prākṣipattattu tathākṣayyau maheṣudhī.
english: Then all those brothers urged Arjuna. So, he threw that (bow) into the water, and similarly, the two inexhaustible great quivers.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 40. Then all those brothers urged Arjuna. So, he threw that (bow) into the water, and similarly, the two inexhaustible great quivers.

sanskrit: ततोऽग्निर्भरतश्रेष्ठ तत्रैवान्तरधीयत ।ययुश्च पाण्डवा वीरास्ततस्ते दक्षिणामुखाः ॥४१॥
transliteration: tato'gnirbharataśreṣṭha tatraivāntaradhīyata ,yayuśca pāṇḍavā vīrāstataste dakṣiṇāmukhāḥ.
english: O best of Bhāratas, then Agni (the fire god) disappeared right there. And then, those heroic Pāṇḍavas went, facing south.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 41. O best of Bhāratas, then Agni (the fire god) disappeared right there. And then, those heroic Pāṇḍavas went, facing south.

sanskrit: ततस्ते तूत्तरेणैव तीरेण लवणाम्भसः ।जग्मुर्भरतशार्दूल दिशं दक्षिणपश्चिमम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: tataste tūttareṇaiva tīreṇa lavaṇāmbhasaḥ ,jagmurbharataśārdūla diśaṁ dakṣiṇapaścimam.
english: O tiger among Bhāratas, then those (Pāṇḍavas) indeed went along the northern shore of the salty ocean towards the southwest direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 42. O tiger among Bhāratas, then those (Pāṇḍavas) indeed went along the northern shore of the salty ocean towards the southwest direction.

sanskrit: ततः पुनः समावृत्ताः पश्चिमां दिशमेव ते ।ददृशुर्द्वारकां चापि सागरेण परिप्लुताम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: tataḥ punaḥ samāvṛttāḥ paścimāṁ diśameva te ,dadṛśurdvārakāṁ cāpi sāgareṇa pariplutām.
english: Then, having returned towards the western direction, they again saw Dvārakā, which was completely submerged by the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 43. Then, having returned towards the western direction, they again saw Dvārakā, which was completely submerged by the ocean.

sanskrit: उदीचीं पुनरावृत्त्य ययुर्भरतसत्तमाः ।प्रादक्षिण्यं चिकीर्षन्तः पृथिव्या योगधर्मिणः ॥४४॥
transliteration: udīcīṁ punarāvṛttya yayurbharatasattamāḥ ,prādakṣiṇyaṁ cikīrṣantaḥ pṛthivyā yogadharmiṇaḥ.
english: The foremost of the Bhāratas, steadfast in their observance of "yoga" (yoga) and their natural law (dharma), again turned northward and proceeded, intending to circumambulate the earth clockwise.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 1 Verse 44. The foremost of the Bhāratas, steadfast in their observance of "yoga" (yoga) and their natural law (dharma), again turned northward and proceeded, intending to circumambulate the earth clockwise.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततस्ते नियतात्मान उदीचीं दिशमास्थिताः ।ददृशुर्योगयुक्ताश्च हिमवन्तं महागिरिम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataste niyatātmāna udīcīṁ diśamāsthitāḥ ,dadṛśuryogayuktāśca himavantaṁ mahāgirim.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then they, with their selves (ātman) controlled and engaged in "yoga" (yoga), having proceeded towards the northern direction, beheld the great mountain Himavat.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then they, with their selves (ātman) controlled and engaged in "yoga" (yoga), having proceeded towards the northern direction, beheld the great mountain Himavat.

sanskrit: तं चाप्यतिक्रमन्तस्ते ददृशुर्वालुकार्णवम् ।अवैक्षन्त महाशैलं मेरुं शिखरिणां वरम् ॥२॥
transliteration: taṁ cāpyatikramantaste dadṛśurvālukārṇavam ,avaikṣanta mahāśailaṁ meruṁ śikhariṇāṁ varam.
english: And surpassing that (Himavat), they also beheld a sandy expanse (vālukārṇava). They then observed the great mountain Meru, the foremost among peaks.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 2. And surpassing that (Himavat), they also beheld a sandy expanse (vālukārṇava). They then observed the great mountain Meru, the foremost among peaks.

sanskrit: तेषां तु गच्छतां शीघ्रं सर्वेषां योगधर्मिणाम् ।याज्ञसेनी भ्रष्टयोगा निपपात महीतले ॥३॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tu gacchatāṁ śīghraṁ sarveṣāṁ yogadharmiṇām ,yājñasenī bhraṣṭayogā nipapāta mahītale.
english: As all of them, observing their spiritual discipline (yoga-dharma), were swiftly proceeding, Yājñaseṇī (Draupadi) fell to the ground, having lost her spiritual discipline.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 3. As all of them, observing their spiritual discipline (yoga-dharma), were swiftly proceeding, Yājñaseṇī (Draupadi) fell to the ground, having lost her spiritual discipline.

sanskrit: तां तु प्रपतितां दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनो महाबलः ।उवाच धर्मराजानं याज्ञसेनीमवेक्ष्य ह ॥४॥
transliteration: tāṁ tu prapatitāṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,uvāca dharmarājānaṁ yājñasenīmavekṣya ha.
english: But the mighty Bhīmasena, having seen her fallen, spoke to King Yudhiṣṭhira after looking at Yājñaseṇī (Draupadi).
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 4. But the mighty Bhīmasena, having seen her fallen, spoke to King Yudhiṣṭhira after looking at Yājñaseṇī (Draupadi).

sanskrit: नाधर्मश्चरितः कश्चिद्राजपुत्र्या परंतप ।कारणं किं नु तद्राजन्यत्कृष्णा पतिता भुवि ॥५॥
transliteration: nādharmaścaritaḥ kaścidrājaputryā paraṁtapa ,kāraṇaṁ kiṁ nu tadrājanyatkṛṣṇā patitā bhuvi.
english: O tormentor of foes, the princess committed no unrighteous act. What, then, O King, is the reason that Kṛṣṇā (Draupadi) has fallen to the ground?
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 5. O tormentor of foes, the princess committed no unrighteous act. What, then, O King, is the reason that Kṛṣṇā (Draupadi) has fallen to the ground?

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।पक्षपातो महानस्या विशेषेण धनंजये ।तस्यैतत्फलमद्यैषा भुङ्क्ते पुरुषसत्तम ॥६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,pakṣapāto mahānasyā viśeṣeṇa dhanaṁjaye ,tasyaitatphalamadyaiṣā bhuṅkte puruṣasattama.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira replied: "She harbored a great partiality, especially for Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna). This is the consequence of that, which she is experiencing today, O best among men."
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 6. Yudhiṣṭhira replied: "She harbored a great partiality, especially for Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna). This is the consequence of that, which she is experiencing today, O best among men."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वानवेक्ष्यैनां ययौ धर्मसुतो नृपः ।समाधाय मनो धीमान्धर्मात्मा पुरुषर्षभः ॥७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvānavekṣyaināṁ yayau dharmasuto nṛpaḥ ,samādhāya mano dhīmāndharmātmā puruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having spoken thus, the wise king Yudhiṣṭhira, the righteous son of dharma (dharma) and best among men, composed his mind and departed without looking at her.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 7. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having spoken thus, the wise king Yudhiṣṭhira, the righteous son of dharma (dharma) and best among men, composed his mind and departed without looking at her.

sanskrit: सहदेवस्ततो धीमान्निपपात महीतले ।तं चापि पतितं दृष्ट्वा भीमो राजानमब्रवीत् ॥८॥
transliteration: sahadevastato dhīmānnipapāta mahītale ,taṁ cāpi patitaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmo rājānamabravīt.
english: Then the wise Sahadeva fell upon the ground. And seeing him fallen, Bhīma spoke to the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 8. Then the wise Sahadeva fell upon the ground. And seeing him fallen, Bhīma spoke to the king.

sanskrit: योऽयमस्मासु सर्वेषु शुश्रूषुरनहंकृतः ।सोऽयं माद्रवतीपुत्रः कस्मान्निपतितो भुवि ॥९॥
transliteration: yo'yamasmāsu sarveṣu śuśrūṣuranahaṁkṛtaḥ ,so'yaṁ mādravatīputraḥ kasmānnipatito bhuvi.
english: This one, who was always eager to serve among all of us and was free from ego (ahaṅkāra) – why has this son of Mādrī fallen on the ground?
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 9. This one, who was always eager to serve among all of us and was free from ego (ahaṅkāra) – why has this son of Mādrī fallen on the ground?

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।आत्मनः सदृशं प्राज्ञं नैषोऽमन्यत कंचन ।तेन दोषेण पतितस्तस्मादेष नृपात्मजः ॥१०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,ātmanaḥ sadṛśaṁ prājñaṁ naiṣo'manyata kaṁcana ,tena doṣeṇa patitastasmādeṣa nṛpātmajaḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "He did not consider anyone equal to his own self (ātman) in wisdom. He has fallen due to that fault. Therefore, this son of the king has fallen."
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 10. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "He did not consider anyone equal to his own self (ātman) in wisdom. He has fallen due to that fault. Therefore, this son of the king has fallen."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा तु समुत्सृज्य सहदेवं ययौ तदा ।भ्रातृभिः सह कौन्तेयः शुना चैव युधिष्ठिरः ॥११॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā tu samutsṛjya sahadevaṁ yayau tadā ,bhrātṛbhiḥ saha kaunteyaḥ śunā caiva yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, then proceeded along with his brothers and the dog, abandoning Sahadeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 11. Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, then proceeded along with his brothers and the dog, abandoning Sahadeva.

sanskrit: कृष्णां निपतितां दृष्ट्वा सहदेवं च पाण्डवम् ।आर्तो बन्धुप्रियः शूरो नकुलो निपपात ह ॥१२॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇāṁ nipatitāṁ dṛṣṭvā sahadevaṁ ca pāṇḍavam ,ārto bandhupriyaḥ śūro nakulo nipapāta ha.
english: Seeing Kṛṣṇā fallen and also Sahadeva, the Pandava, the distressed, kinsman-loving, heroic Nakula indeed fell down.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 12. Seeing Kṛṣṇā fallen and also Sahadeva, the Pandava, the distressed, kinsman-loving, heroic Nakula indeed fell down.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्निपतिते वीरे नकुले चारुदर्शने ।पुनरेव तदा भीमो राजानमिदमब्रवीत् ॥१३॥

transliteration: anṛtaṁ na smarāmyasya svaireṣvapi mahātmanaḥ ,atha kasya vikāro'yaṁ yenāyaṁ patito bhuvi.
english: I do not recall this great-souled one (ātman) ever speaking a lie, even in these his own private matters. So, whose fault is this, by which he has fallen to the ground?

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।एकाह्ना निर्दहेयं वै शत्रूनित्यर्जुनोऽब्रवीत् ।न च तत्कृतवानेष शूरमानी ततोऽपतत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,ekāhnā nirdaheyaṁ vai śatrūnityarjuno'bravīt ,na ca tatkṛtavāneṣa śūramānī tato'patat.
english: Yudhishthira said, "Arjuna indeed declared, 'I would incinerate all enemies in a single day.' But this proud one, considering himself a hero, did not accomplish that, and therefore he fell."

sanskrit: अवमेने धनुर्ग्राहानेष सर्वांश्च फल्गुनः ।यथा चोक्तं तथा चैव कर्तव्यं भूतिमिच्छता ॥२२॥
transliteration: avamene dhanurgrāhāneṣa sarvāṁśca phalgunaḥ ,yathā coktaṁ tathā caiva kartavyaṁ bhūtimicchatā.
english: This Phalguna (Arjuna) scorned all other bow-wielders. As was declared, so indeed should it be done by one desiring prosperity.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा प्रस्थितो राजा भीमोऽथ निपपात ह ।पतितश्चाब्रवीद्भीमो धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā prasthito rājā bhīmo'tha nipapāta ha ,patitaścābravīdbhīmo dharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, the departing King Bhīma then indeed fell down. And having fallen, Bhīma spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira, the king upholding natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 23. Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, the departing King Bhīma then indeed fell down. And having fallen, Bhīma spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira, the king upholding natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: भो भो राजन्नवेक्षस्व पतितोऽहं प्रियस्तव ।किंनिमित्तं च पतनं ब्रूहि मे यदि वेत्थ ह ॥२४॥
transliteration: bho bho rājannavekṣasva patito'haṁ priyastava ,kiṁnimittaṁ ca patanaṁ brūhi me yadi vettha ha.
english: O King, O King! Look at me, your dear one, I have fallen. Tell me the reason for this fall, if you indeed know it.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 24. O King, O King! Look at me, your dear one, I have fallen. Tell me the reason for this fall, if you indeed know it.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।अतिभुक्तं च भवता प्राणेन च विकत्थसे ।अनवेक्ष्य परं पार्थ तेनासि पतितः क्षितौ ॥२५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,atibhuktaṁ ca bhavatā prāṇena ca vikatthase ,anavekṣya paraṁ pārtha tenāsi patitaḥ kṣitau.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "You ate excessively, and you boasted of your strength. O Pārtha, because you disregarded others, you have fallen on the earth."
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 25. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "You ate excessively, and you boasted of your strength. O Pārtha, because you disregarded others, you have fallen on the earth."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा तं महाबाहुर्जगामानवलोकयन् ।श्वा त्वेकोऽनुययौ यस्ते बहुशः कीर्तितो मया ॥२६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā taṁ mahābāhurjagāmānavalokayan ,śvā tveko'nuyayau yaste bahuśaḥ kīrtito mayā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, the mighty-armed Yudhiṣṭhira went on without looking back at him. But one dog followed, which I have repeatedly mentioned to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 2 Verse 26. Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, the mighty-armed Yudhiṣṭhira went on without looking back at him. But one dog followed, which I have repeatedly mentioned to you.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः संनादयञ्शक्रो दिवं भूमिं च सर्वशः ।रथेनोपययौ पार्थमारोहेत्यब्रवीच्च तम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ saṁnādayañśakro divaṁ bhūmiṁ ca sarvaśaḥ ,rathenopayayau pārthamārohetyabravīcca tam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then Indra (Śakra), making the sky and earth resound completely, approached Pārtha (Arjuna) on his chariot and told him, 'Ascend!'
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then Indra (Śakra), making the sky and earth resound completely, approached Pārtha (Arjuna) on his chariot and told him, 'Ascend!'

sanskrit: स भ्रातॄन्पतितान्दृष्ट्वा धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।अब्रवीच्छोकसंतप्तः सहस्राक्षमिदं वचः ॥२॥
transliteration: sa bhrātṝnpatitāndṛṣṭvā dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,abravīcchokasaṁtaptaḥ sahasrākṣamidaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Seeing his brothers fallen, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), overwhelmed by sorrow, spoke these words to Sahasrākṣa (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 2. Seeing his brothers fallen, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), overwhelmed by sorrow, spoke these words to Sahasrākṣa (Indra).

sanskrit: भ्रातरः पतिता मेऽत्र आगच्छेयुर्मया सह ।न विना भ्रातृभिः स्वर्गमिच्छे गन्तुं सुरेश्वर ॥३॥
transliteration: bhrātaraḥ patitā me'tra āgaccheyurmayā saha ,na vinā bhrātṛbhiḥ svargamicche gantuṁ sureśvara.
english: My brothers, who have fallen here, should come with me. I do not wish to go to heaven without my brothers, O Lord of gods (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 3. My brothers, who have fallen here, should come with me. I do not wish to go to heaven without my brothers, O Lord of gods (Indra).

sanskrit: सुकुमारी सुखार्हा च राजपुत्री पुरंदर ।सास्माभिः सह गच्छेत तद्भवाननुमन्यताम् ॥४॥
transliteration: sukumārī sukhārhā ca rājaputrī puraṁdara ,sāsmābhiḥ saha gaccheta tadbhavānanumanyatām.
english: And the princess (Draupadī), delicate and accustomed to comfort, O Purandara (Indra), should go with us. Please approve of that.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 4. And the princess (Draupadī), delicate and accustomed to comfort, O Purandara (Indra), should go with us. Please approve of that.

sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।भ्रातॄन्द्रक्ष्यसि पुत्रांस्त्वमग्रतस्त्रिदिवं गतान् ।कृष्णया सहितान्सर्वान्मा शुचो भरतर्षभ ॥५॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,bhrātṝndrakṣyasi putrāṁstvamagratastridivaṁ gatān ,kṛṣṇayā sahitānsarvānmā śuco bharatarṣabha.
english: Indra said: 'O best among the Bharatas, you will see your brothers and sons who have gone to heaven before you, all of them accompanied by Draupadi (kṛṣṇā). Do not grieve.'
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 5. Indra said: 'O best among the Bharatas, you will see your brothers and sons who have gone to heaven before you, all of them accompanied by Draupadi (kṛṣṇā). Do not grieve.'

sanskrit: निक्षिप्य मानुषं देहं गतास्ते भरतर्षभ ।अनेन त्वं शरीरेण स्वर्गं गन्ता न संशयः ॥६॥
transliteration: nikṣipya mānuṣaṁ dehaṁ gatāste bharatarṣabha ,anena tvaṁ śarīreṇa svargaṁ gantā na saṁśayaḥ.
english: O best among the Bharatas, they have cast off their human bodies and departed. You, with this very body, will go to heaven; there is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 6. O best among the Bharatas, they have cast off their human bodies and departed. You, with this very body, will go to heaven; there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।अयं श्वा भूतभव्येश भक्तो मां नित्यमेव ह ।स गच्छेत मया सार्धमानृशंस्या हि मे मतिः ॥७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,ayaṁ śvā bhūtabhavyeśa bhakto māṁ nityameva ha ,sa gaccheta mayā sārdhamānṛśaṁsyā hi me matiḥ.
english: Yudhishthira said: 'O Lord of past and future, this dog has always been devoted to me. He should indeed go with me, for compassion (ānṛśaṃsya) is my inclination.'
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 7. Yudhishthira said: 'O Lord of past and future, this dog has always been devoted to me. He should indeed go with me, for compassion (ānṛśaṃsya) is my inclination.'

sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।अमर्त्यत्वं मत्समत्वं च राजञ्श्रियं कृत्स्नां महतीं चैव कीर्तिम् ।संप्राप्तोऽद्य स्वर्गसुखानि च त्वं त्यज श्वानं नात्र नृशंसमस्ति ॥८॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,amartyatvaṁ matsamatvaṁ ca rāja;ñśriyaṁ kṛtsnāṁ mahatīṁ caiva kīrtim ,saṁprāpto'dya svargasukhāni ca tvaṁ; tyaja śvānaṁ nātra nṛśaṁsamasti.
english: Indra said: 'O king, you have today attained immortality, equality with me, complete and great glory, and also heavenly pleasures. Abandon the dog; there is no cruelty (nṛśaṃsa) in this.'
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 8. Indra said: 'O king, you have today attained immortality, equality with me, complete and great glory, and also heavenly pleasures. Abandon the dog; there is no cruelty (nṛśaṃsa) in this.'

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।अनार्यमार्येण सहस्रनेत्र शक्यं कर्तुं दुष्करमेतदार्य ।मा मे श्रिया संगमनं तयास्तु यस्याः कृते भक्तजनं त्यजेयम् ॥९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,anāryamāryeṇa sahasranetra; śakyaṁ kartuṁ duṣkarametadārya ,mā me śriyā saṁgamanaṁ tayāstu; yasyāḥ kṛte bhaktajanaṁ tyajeyam.
english: Yudhishthira said: "O thousand-eyed Indra, it is an ignoble act, though difficult, for a noble person to perform. May I not attain prosperity (śrī) through an act for the sake of which I would abandon a loyal companion."
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 9. Yudhishthira said: "O thousand-eyed Indra, it is an ignoble act, though difficult, for a noble person to perform. May I not attain prosperity (śrī) through an act for the sake of which I would abandon a loyal companion."

sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।स्वर्गे लोके श्ववतां नास्ति धिष्ण्यमिष्टापूर्तं क्रोधवशा हरन्ति ।ततो विचार्य क्रियतां धर्मराज त्यज श्वानं नात्र नृशंसमस्ति ॥१०॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,svarge loke śvavatāṁ nāsti dhiṣṇya;miṣṭāpūrtaṁ krodhavaśā haranti ,tato vicārya kriyatāṁ dharmarāja; tyaja śvānaṁ nātra nṛśaṁsamasti.
english: Indra said: "In the celestial world (svarga loka), there is no abode for those with dogs. Their angry presence nullifies the merit acquired from Vedic rituals (iṣṭa) and charitable deeds (pūrta). Therefore, O king of righteousness (dharma-rāja), consider this and abandon the dog; there is no act of cruelty (nṛśaṃsa) in doing so."
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 10. Indra said: "In the celestial world (svarga loka), there is no abode for those with dogs. Their angry presence nullifies the merit acquired from Vedic rituals (iṣṭa) and charitable deeds (pūrta). Therefore, O king of righteousness (dharma-rāja), consider this and abandon the dog; there is no act of cruelty (nṛśaṃsa) in doing so."

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।भक्तत्यागं प्राहुरत्यन्तपापं तुल्यं लोके ब्रह्मवध्याकृतेन ।तस्मान्नाहं जातु कथंचनाद्य त्यक्ष्याम्येनं स्वसुखार्थी महेन्द्र ॥११॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,bhaktatyāgaṁ prāhuratyantapāpaṁ; tulyaṁ loke brahmavadhyākṛtena ,tasmānnāhaṁ jātu kathaṁcanādya; tyakṣyāmyenaṁ svasukhārthī mahendra.
english: Yudhishthira said: "Abandoning a loyal companion is proclaimed to be an extremely grave sin (pāpa), equivalent in the world to the act of slaying a brahmin. Therefore, O great Indra, I will never, by any means, abandon him today for the sake of my own happiness."
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 11. Yudhishthira said: "Abandoning a loyal companion is proclaimed to be an extremely grave sin (pāpa), equivalent in the world to the act of slaying a brahmin. sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाच ।शुना दृष्टं क्रोधवशा हरन्ति यद्दत्तमिष्टं विवृतमथो हुतं च ।तस्माच्छुनस्त्यागमिमं कुरुष्व शुनस्त्यागात्प्राप्स्यसे देवलोकम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: indra uvāca ,śunā dṛṣṭaṁ krodhavaśā haranti; yaddattamiṣṭaṁ vivṛtamatho hutaṁ ca ,tasmācchunastyāgamimaṁ kuruṣva; śunastyāgātprāpsyase devalokam.
english: Indra said: "Whatever has been given, offered in a Vedic ritual (iṣṭa), publicly declared, or offered into the fire (huta) – all of it, when seen by a dog, is seized and rendered ineffective by their inherent nature of anger. Therefore, you must abandon this dog. By abandoning the dog, you will attain the realm of the gods (devaloka)."
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 12. Indra said: "Whatever has been given, offered in a Vedic ritual (iṣṭa), publicly declared, or offered into the fire (huta) – all of it, when seen by a dog, is seized and rendered ineffective by their inherent nature of anger. Therefore, you must abandon this dog. By abandoning the dog, you will attain the realm of the gods (devaloka)."

sanskrit: त्यक्त्वा भ्रातॄन्दयितां चापि कृष्णां प्राप्तो लोकः कर्मणा स्वेन वीर ।श्वानं चैनं न त्यजसे कथं नु त्यागं कृत्स्नं चास्थितो मुह्यसेऽद्य ॥१३॥
transliteration: tyaktvā bhrātṝndayitāṁ cāpi kṛṣṇāṁ; prāpto lokaḥ karmaṇā svena vīra ,śvānaṁ cainaṁ na tyajase kathaṁ nu; tyāgaṁ kṛtsnaṁ cāsthito muhyase'dya.
english: O hero, having abandoned your brothers and even your beloved Krishnaa (Draupadi), you achieved your goal (loka) by your own deeds (karma). Why, then, having embraced complete renunciation (tyāga), do you not forsake this dog? And why are you bewildered now?
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 13. O hero, having abandoned your brothers and even your beloved Krishnaa (Draupadi), you achieved your goal (loka) by your own deeds (karma). Why, then, having embraced complete renunciation (tyāga), do you not forsake this dog? And why are you bewildered now?

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।न विद्यते संधिरथापि विग्रहो मृतैर्मर्त्यैरिति लोकेषु निष्ठा ।न ते मया जीवयितुं हि शक्या तस्मात्त्यागस्तेषु कृतो न जीवताम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,na vidyate saṁdhirathāpi vigraho; mṛtairmartyairiti lokeṣu niṣṭhā ,na te mayā jīvayituṁ hi śakyā; tasmāttyāgasteṣu kṛto na jīvatām.
english: Yudhishthira said: "There is no possibility of alliance or conflict with dead mortals; this is an established truth in the world. Indeed, I cannot bring them back to life. Therefore, my abandonment was directed towards them, as they were not among the living."
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 14. Yudhishthira said: "There is no possibility of alliance or conflict with dead mortals; this is an established truth in the world. Indeed, I cannot bring them back to life. Therefore, my abandonment was directed towards them, as they were not among the living."

sanskrit: प्रतिप्रदानं शरणागतस्य स्त्रिया वधो ब्राह्मणस्वापहारः ।मित्रद्रोहस्तानि चत्वारि शक्र भक्तत्यागश्चैव समो मतो मे ॥१५॥
transliteration: pratipradānaṁ śaraṇāgatasya; striyā vadho brāhmaṇasvāpahāraḥ ,mitradrohastāni catvāri śakra; bhaktatyāgaścaiva samo mato me.
english: O Indra (śakra), abandoning one who has sought refuge (śaraṇāgata), the killing of a woman, misappropriating the property of a brahmin, and betraying a friend - these four (sins), and indeed the abandonment of a devotee (bhakta), are considered equal by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 15. O Indra (śakra), abandoning one who has sought refuge (śaraṇāgata), the killing of a woman, misappropriating the property of a brahmin, and betraying a friend - these four (sins), and indeed the abandonment of a devotee (bhakta), are considered equal by me.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तद्धर्मराजस्य वचो निशम्य धर्मस्वरूपी भगवानुवाच ।युधिष्ठिरं प्रीतियुक्तो नरेन्द्रं श्लक्ष्णैर्वाक्यैः संस्तवसंप्रयुक्तैः ॥१६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,taddharmarājasya vaco niśamya; dharmasvarūpī bhagavānuvāca ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ prītiyukto narendraṁ; ślakṣṇairvākyaiḥ saṁstavasaṁprayuktaiḥ.
english: Vaishampayana said: "Having heard those words of Yudhishthira, the king of natural law (dharma-rāja), the revered one (bhagavān), who embodied (dharma), being filled with affection, spoke to King Yudhishthira with gentle words accompanied by praise."
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 16. Vaishampayana said: "Having heard those words of Yudhishthira, the king of natural law (dharma-rāja), the revered one (bhagavān), who embodied (dharma), being filled with affection, spoke to King Yudhishthira with gentle words accompanied by praise."

sanskrit: अभिजातोऽसि राजेन्द्र पितुर्वृत्तेन मेधया ।अनुक्रोशेन चानेन सर्वभूतेषु भारत ॥१७॥
transliteration: abhijāto'si rājendra piturvṛttena medhayā ,anukrośena cānena sarvabhūteṣu bhārata.
english: O best of kings (rājendra), O Bhārata, you are truly nobly born because of your father's character, your intelligence, and this compassion you have for all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 17. O best of kings (rājendra), O Bhārata, you are truly nobly born because of your father's character, your intelligence, and this compassion you have for all beings.

sanskrit: पुरा द्वैतवने चासि मया पुत्र परीक्षितः ।पानीयार्थे पराक्रान्ता यत्र ते भ्रातरो हताः ॥१८॥
transliteration: purā dvaitavane cāsi mayā putra parīkṣitaḥ ,pānīyārthe parākrāntā yatra te bhrātaro hatāḥ.
english: O son, you were tested by me long ago in the Dvaita forest, where your brothers, having struggled for water, lay struck down.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 18. O son, you were tested by me long ago in the Dvaita forest, where your brothers, having struggled for water, lay struck down.

sanskrit: भीमार्जुनौ परित्यज्य यत्र त्वं भ्रातरावुभौ ।मात्रोः साम्यमभीप्सन्वै नकुलं जीवमिच्छसि ॥१९॥
transliteration: bhīmārjunau parityajya yatra tvaṁ bhrātarāvubhau ,mātroḥ sāmyamabhīpsanvai nakulaṁ jīvamicchasi.
english: There, desiring equality for both mothers, you chose Nakula to live, forsaking both brothers, Bhīma and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 19. There, desiring equality for both mothers, you chose Nakula to live, forsaking both brothers, Bhīma and Arjuna.

sanskrit: अयं श्वा भक्त इत्येव त्यक्तो देवरथस्त्वया ।तस्मात्स्वर्गे न ते तुल्यः कश्चिदस्ति नराधिप ॥२०॥
transliteration: ayaṁ śvā bhakta ityeva tyakto devarathastvayā ,tasmātsvarge na te tulyaḥ kaścidasti narādhipa.
english: You abandoned the divine chariot simply because you recognized, 'this dog is devoted.' Therefore, O ruler of men, no one in heaven is comparable to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 20. You abandoned the divine chariot simply because you recognized, 'this dog is devoted.' Therefore, O ruler of men, no one in heaven is comparable to you.

sanskrit: अतस्तवाक्षया लोकाः स्वशरीरेण भारत ।प्राप्तोऽसि भरतश्रेष्ठ दिव्यां गतिमनुत्तमाम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: atastavākṣayā lokāḥ svaśarīreṇa bhārata ,prāpto'si bharataśreṣṭha divyāṁ gatimanuttamām.
english: Therefore, O descendant of Bhārata, you have attained imperishable realms in your own body. O best of the Bhāratas, you have achieved an unsurpassed divine destination.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 21. Therefore, O descendant of Bhārata, you have attained imperishable realms in your own body. O best of the Bhāratas, you have achieved an unsurpassed divine destination.

sanskrit: ततो धर्मश्च शक्रश्च मरुतश्चाश्विनावपि ।देवा देवर्षयश्चैव रथमारोप्य पाण्डवम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tato dharmaśca śakraśca marutaścāśvināvapi ,devā devarṣayaścaiva rathamāropya pāṇḍavam.
english: Then, the god of natural law (dharma), Śakra (Indra), the Maruts, and the two Aśvins, as well as the gods and divine sages, having placed the son of Pāṇḍu (Yudhiṣṭhira) upon a chariot...
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 22. Then, the god of natural law (dharma), Śakra (Indra), the Maruts, and the two Aśvins, as well as the gods and divine sages, having placed the son of Pāṇḍu (Yudhiṣṭhira) upon a chariot...

sanskrit: प्रययुः स्वैर्विमानैस्ते सिद्धाः कामविहारिणः ।सर्वे विरजसः पुण्याः पुण्यवाग्बुद्धिकर्मिणः ॥२३॥
transliteration: prayayuḥ svairvimānaiste siddhāḥ kāmavihāriṇaḥ ,sarve virajasaḥ puṇyāḥ puṇyavāgbuddhikarmiṇaḥ.
english: ...they departed in their own celestial vehicles – these perfected beings who move at will. All were free from impurity, virtuous, and acted with pure speech, intellect, and deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 23. ...they departed in their own celestial vehicles – these perfected beings who move at will. All were free from impurity, virtuous, and acted with pure speech, intellect, and deeds.

sanskrit: स तं रथं समास्थाय राजा कुरुकुलोद्वहः ।ऊर्ध्वमाचक्रमे शीघ्रं तेजसावृत्य रोदसी ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa taṁ rathaṁ samāsthāya rājā kurukulodvahaḥ ,ūrdhvamācakrame śīghraṁ tejasāvṛtya rodasī.
english: Having mounted that chariot, he, the king, the upholder of the Kuru lineage, quickly ascended upwards, enveloping both heaven and earth with his splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 24. Having mounted that chariot, he, the king, the upholder of the Kuru lineage, quickly ascended upwards, enveloping both heaven and earth with his splendor.

sanskrit: ततो देवनिकायस्थो नारदः सर्वलोकवित् ।उवाचोच्चैस्तदा वाक्यं बृहद्वादी बृहत्तपाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tato devanikāyastho nāradaḥ sarvalokavit ,uvācoccaistadā vākyaṁ bṛhadvādī bṛhattapāḥ.
english: Then, Narada, who was present in the assembly of gods, a knower of all worlds, a great orator, and one possessing immense austerities (tapas), spoke loudly.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 25. Then, Narada, who was present in the assembly of gods, a knower of all worlds, a great orator, and one possessing immense austerities (tapas), spoke loudly.

sanskrit: येऽपि राजर्षयः सर्वे ते चापि समुपस्थिताः ।कीर्तिं प्रच्छाद्य तेषां वै कुरुराजोऽधितिष्ठति ॥२६॥
transliteration: ye'pi rājarṣayaḥ sarve te cāpi samupasthitāḥ ,kīrtiṁ pracchādya teṣāṁ vai kururājo'dhitiṣṭhati.
english: Indeed, the King of the Kurus (Yudhishthira) surpasses all those royal sages who are present, by overshadowing their fame.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 26. Indeed, the King of the Kurus (Yudhishthira) surpasses all those royal sages who are present, by overshadowing their fame.

sanskrit: लोकानावृत्य यशसा तेजसा वृत्तसंपदा ।स्वशरीरेण संप्राप्तं नान्यं शुश्रुम पाण्डवात् ॥२७॥
transliteration: lokānāvṛtya yaśasā tejasā vṛttasaṁpadā ,svaśarīreṇa saṁprāptaṁ nānyaṁ śuśruma pāṇḍavāt.
english: We have not heard of anyone other than the Pandava who, by his own person, pervaded the worlds with such fame, splendor, and abundance of good conduct.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 27. We have not heard of anyone other than the Pandava who, by his own person, pervaded the worlds with such fame, splendor, and abundance of good conduct.

sanskrit: नारदस्य वचः श्रुत्वा राजा वचनमब्रवीत् ।देवानामन्त्र्य धर्मात्मा स्वपक्षांश्चैव पार्थिवान् ॥२८॥
transliteration: nāradasya vacaḥ śrutvā rājā vacanamabravīt ,devānāmantrya dharmātmā svapakṣāṁścaiva pārthivān.
english: Having heard Narada's speech, the king, who possessed a righteous nature (dharma), spoke, addressing both the gods and his own allied kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 28. Having heard Narada's speech, the king, who possessed a righteous nature (dharma), spoke, addressing both the gods and his own allied kings.

sanskrit: शुभं वा यदि वा पापं भ्रातॄणां स्थानमद्य मे ।तदेव प्राप्तुमिच्छामि लोकानन्यान्न कामये ॥२९॥
transliteration: śubhaṁ vā yadi vā pāpaṁ bhrātṝṇāṁ sthānamadya me ,tadeva prāptumicchāmi lokānanyānna kāmaye.
english: Whether it is good or bad, the abode of my brothers is mine today. I desire to attain that very place; I do not wish for any other worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 29. Whether it is good or bad, the abode of my brothers is mine today. I desire to attain that very place; I do not wish for any other worlds.

sanskrit: राज्ञस्तु वचनं श्रुत्वा देवराजः पुरंदरः ।आनृशंस्यसमायुक्तं प्रत्युवाच युधिष्ठिरम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: rājñastu vacanaṁ śrutvā devarājaḥ puraṁdaraḥ ,ānṛśaṁsyasamāyuktaṁ pratyuvāca yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Having heard the words of the king, the chief of the gods (devarāja), Purandara (Indra), filled with compassion, replied to Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 30. Having heard the words of the king, the chief of the gods (devarāja), Purandara (Indra), filled with compassion, replied to Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: स्थानेऽस्मिन्वस राजेन्द्र कर्मभिर्निर्जिते शुभैः ।किं त्वं मानुष्यकं स्नेहमद्यापि परिकर्षसि ॥३१॥
transliteration: sthāne'sminvasa rājendra karmabhirnirjite śubhaiḥ ,kiṁ tvaṁ mānuṣyakaṁ snehamadyāpi parikarṣasi.
english: O king (rājendra), reside in this realm, which has been won through virtuous deeds (karma). Why do you still cling to human affection even now?
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 31. O king (rājendra), reside in this realm, which has been won through virtuous deeds (karma). Why do you still cling to human affection even now?

sanskrit: सिद्धिं प्राप्तोऽसि परमां यथा नान्यः पुमान्क्वचित् ।नैव ते भ्रातरः स्थानं संप्राप्ताः कुरुनन्दन ॥३२॥
transliteration: siddhiṁ prāpto'si paramāṁ yathā nānyaḥ pumānkvacit ,naiva te bhrātaraḥ sthānaṁ saṁprāptāḥ kurunandana.
english: You have attained the supreme accomplishment (siddhi), such as no other person has ever achieved anywhere. Indeed, your brothers, O scion of the Kurus (kurunandana), have not reached this realm.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 32. You have attained the supreme accomplishment (siddhi), such as no other person has ever achieved anywhere. Indeed, your brothers, O scion of the Kurus (kurunandana), have not reached this realm.

sanskrit: अद्यापि मानुषो भावः स्पृशते त्वां नराधिप ।स्वर्गोऽयं पश्य देवर्षीन्सिद्धांश्च त्रिदिवालयान् ॥३३॥
transliteration: adyāpi mānuṣo bhāvaḥ spṛśate tvāṁ narādhipa ,svargo'yaṁ paśya devarṣīnsiddhāṁśca tridivālayān.
english: Even now, a human sentiment affects you, O ruler of men. This is heaven; behold the divine sages and the perfected beings residing in the celestial realms.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 33. Even now, a human sentiment affects you, O ruler of men. This is heaven; behold the divine sages and the perfected beings residing in the celestial realms.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्तु देवेन्द्रमेवंवादिनमीश्वरम् ।पुनरेवाब्रवीद्धीमानिदं वचनमर्थवत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastu devendramevaṁvādinamīśvaram ,punarevābravīddhīmānidaṁ vacanamarthavat.
english: However, the wise Yudhishthira again spoke these meaningful words to Devendra, the lord, who was speaking in this manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 34. However, the wise Yudhishthira again spoke these meaningful words to Devendra, the lord, who was speaking in this manner.

sanskrit: तैर्विना नोत्सहे वस्तुमिह दैत्यनिबर्हण ।गन्तुमिच्छामि तत्राहं यत्र मे भ्रातरो गताः ॥३५॥
transliteration: tairvinā notsahe vastumiha daityanibarhaṇa ,gantumicchāmi tatrāhaṁ yatra me bhrātaro gatāḥ.
english: Without them, I am unable to stay here, O destroyer of demons. I desire to go where my brothers have gone.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 35. Without them, I am unable to stay here, O destroyer of demons. I desire to go where my brothers have gone.

sanskrit: यत्र सा बृहती श्यामा बुद्धिसत्त्वगुणान्विता ।द्रौपदी योषितां श्रेष्ठा यत्र चैव प्रिया मम ॥३६॥
transliteration: yatra sā bṛhatī śyāmā buddhisattvaguṇānvitā ,draupadī yoṣitāṁ śreṣṭhā yatra caiva priyā mama.
english: Where that tall, dark-skinned, and excellent Draupadi, endowed with intelligence and the quality of goodness (sattva), the foremost among women, is; and where my beloved is.
search_text: Mahabharata Mahāprasthānika Parva Chapter 3 Verse 36. Where that tall, dark-skinned, and excellent Draupadi, endowed with intelligence and the quality of goodness (sattva), the foremost among women, is; and where my beloved is.
